response.content: List of Michelin-starred restaurants in California - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia The French Laundry, a 3 Michelin-starred restaurant in Yountville, California As of the 2025 Michelin Guide , there are 87 restaurants in California that hold a Michelin star. [ 1 ] The Michelin Guides have been published by the French tire company Michelin since 1900. They were designed as a guide to tell drivers about eateries they recommended to visit and to subtly sponsor their tires, by encouraging drivers to use their cars more and therefore need to replace the tires as they wore out. Over time, the stars that were given out started to become more valuable. [ 2 ] Multiple anonymous Michelin inspectors visit the restaurants several times. They rate the restaurants on five criteria: "quality of products", "mastery of flavor and cooking techniques", "the personality of the chef represented in the dining experience", "value for money", and "consistency between inspectors' visits". [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Inspectors have at least ten years of expertise and create a list of popular restaurants supported by media reports, reviews, and diner popularity. If they reach a consensus, Michelin awards restaurants from one to three stars based on its evaluation methodology: One star means "high-quality cooking, worth a stop", two stars signify "excellent cooking, worth a detour", and three stars denote "exceptional cuisine, worth a special journey". [ 4 ] The stars are not permanent and restaurants are constantly being re-evaluated. If the criteria are not met, the restaurant will lose its stars. [ 2 ] The California Guide came about in 2019 as the result of the merger of two other ones; San Francisco and Los Angeles. [ 5 ] The San Francisco Michelin Guide was the second North American city chosen to have its own Michelin Guide beginning in 2007. Unlike the other U.S. guides which focus mainly in the city proper, the San Francisco guide included all the major cities in th

response.title: Protege | Palo Alto, California
response.snippet: Protégé is an upscale casual restaurant featuring New American cuisine and a comprehensive wine & spirits program. The vision of Master Sommelier, Dennis Kelly ...
response.content: Protege | Palo Alto, California item item item item item item item item Welcome Protégé is an upscale casual restaurant featuring New American cuisine and a comprehensive wine & spirits program. The vision of Master Sommelier, Dennis Kelly and Chef Anthony Secviar, Protégé brings the attention to detail found in the finest restaurants to a casual setting in Palo Alto, California. About Protégé offers two distinct dining experiences: the Lounge and the Dining Room. The Lounge offers an à la carte menu in a casual atmosphere—anchored by our 10-seat bar. The Dining Room features a 7-course tasting menu in a comfortable and intimate setting. HOURS Tuesday thru Saturday: 5:00PM – 9:15PM Closed on Sunday and Monday LOCATION 250 California Avenue Palo Alto, CA 94306 CONTACT Protégé does not utilize phone service; to contact the restaurant directly, please email reservations@protegepaloalto.com. response.snippet: Try one of our one-star restaurants during your visit to the Peninsula. Savor innovative tasting menus at Protégé, or indulge in farm-to-table delights at The ...
response.content: Michelin Star Restaurants on The San Francisco Peninsula Your browser is not supported for this experience. We recommend using Chrome, Firefox, Edge, or Safari. Skip navigation Skip to main content Share Dine with MICHELIN-recognition Treat your palate to the exceptional cuisine served by MICHELIN-recognized restaurants on the San Francisco Peninsula. Discover internationally renowned establishments recognized for their culinary artistry along with moderately priced hidden gems showcasing fresh, seasonal ingredients. Whether you dream of an indulgent omakase experience where the chef orchestrates your meal or a relaxed farm-to-table feast under the California sun, you’ll find a dazzling array of local restaurants awarded a one-star, Bib Gourmand, or recommendation. What Each MICHELIN Star Means 3 stars Exceptional cuisine worth a special journey 2 stars Excellent food worth a detour 1 star High-quality cooking worth a stop Bib Gourmand Friendly establishments that serve good food at moderate prices MICHELIN Recommended Recommended Simply serves good food Green Star Restaurants that demonstrate exceptional sustainable and eco-friendly culinary practices. A Taste of The San Francisco Peninsula MICHELIN-recognized restaurants on the Peninsula provide something for every taste and budget. Here, you’ll find one-star fine dining establishments with dazzling prix-fixe menus as well as casual bistros and pubs serving wallet-conscious quality food. Try one of our one-star restaurants during your visit to the Peninsula. Savor innovative tasting menus at Protégé , or indulge in farm-to-table delights at The Village Pub. Dine on Wakuriya's 9-course meal featuring fresh seafood, or experience timeless American continental cuisine at Selby's . Beyond the stars, you’ll find plenty of local restaurants awarded the Bib Gourmand or recommended by MICHELIN.
